_meta.purpose: Template framework pack — generated by Claude as a starting point for onboarding a new target framework. Replace every YOUR_* placeholder with real values. This file teaches the converter's Tier-1 adapter and Tier-3 LLM what the target framework looks like. See FRAMEWORK_AUTHORING.md for the full add/delete process.

Graph.notes: Replace with the target framework's primary orchestration primitive. Example: if the framework uses a Pipeline class, put Pipeline here.

SingleAgent.notes: Replace with the framework's single-agent primitive (if different from the orchestration primitive).

shared_state.notes: Replace with how the framework passes state between steps. If the framework uses a dataclass or dict, describe that pattern here.

checkpointing.notes: Replace with the framework's persistence mechanism. Remove this section if the framework has no checkpointing.

step.return_convention: Describe how outputs are passed to the next step (return value, context mutation, message passing, etc.).
step.notes: Replace with how the framework defines individual processing steps.

final_step.notes: Replace with how the framework signals the end of a workflow / produces the final output.

sequential.notes: Replace with the framework's unconditional sequential wiring.

conditional.notes: Replace with the framework's conditional branching. If the framework uses a different pattern (e.g. if/else in a router method), describe that here.
conditional.pattern:
.add_step(validate, next_step=approve,  condition=lambda ctx: ctx.passed)
.add_step(validate, next_step=retry,   condition=lambda ctx: not ctx.passed)

loop.notes: Replace with how the framework models cycles/loops. Always add a termination condition.

parallel.notes: Replace with the framework's fan-out/fan-in pattern. Remove if not supported.

@tool.registration: Describe how tools are registered with an agent (e.g. tools=[fn] in constructor, or plugin registration).
@tool.notes: Replace with the target framework's tool decoration pattern. If the framework uses class-based tools, describe the class pattern here.

hitl.reality: Replace this section with the framework's real HITL story. If the framework has native pause/resume (like LangGraph interrupt() or MAF RequestInfoExecutor), describe it. If it does not, describe the approved workaround (e.g. human-input tool, host-loop break).

workaround.notes: If the framework has no native HITL, model it as a special tool that the agent calls when a human decision is needed.

hitl.anti_pattern: Do NOT emit framework-specific interrupt primitives that don't exist in this framework.

sync.notes: Replace with how the framework's workflow/agent is invoked.

async.notes: Replace with the async invocation pattern. Remove if not supported.

stream.notes: Replace with the streaming invocation pattern. Remove if not supported.

providers.notes: Replace with the LLM provider story for this framework. Describe how to swap providers.

reject_list.description: Any generated output containing the following is a conversion error for this target.
items: stdlib module in requirements.txt (json, os, io, re, math, typing)
